metadata.summary: Model space explorer for inference and prediction

documentation:
[![build](https://github.com/ihmeuw-msca/modrover/workflows/build/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ihmeuw-msca/modrover/actions)

# ModRover: Model Space Explorer

## Installation

```shell
pip install git+https://github.com/ihmeuw-msca/modrover.git@main
```

## Documentation

https://ihmeuw-msca.github.io/modrover/

classes.name: modrover.exceptions.NotFittedError
documentation: Raised if a user tries to call predict on an unfitted learner/rover.

classes.name: modrover.exceptions.InvalidConfigurationError
documentation: Raised if a provided configuration parameter violates a rule.

classes.name: modrover.strategies.backward.Backward

methods.name: modrover.strategies.backward.Backward.get_next_layer
documentation:
The backward strategy will select a set of learner IDs numbering one
less. This function will return next set of learner ids corresponding
the learers that need to be fitted.

E.g. if the full set of ids is 1-5, and our current is (0, 1, 2)

The downstreams will be (0, 1), (0, 2)

Parameters
----------
curr_layer
    Current explored set of learner ids.
learners
    A dictionary contains all fitted learners.
min_improvement
    Minimum performance improvement requirement for the learner to be
    consider qualified to generate the next layer.
max_len
    Maximum number of learner in each layer.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.backward.Backward._get_upstream_learner_ids
documentation:
Return the possible previous nodes.
For Backward, this is the current learner id's children.

Backward strategy starts from the model with all the covariates that we
want to explore and explores backward with less and less covaraites base on
the learner performance.

Parameters
----------
num_covs
    Number of exploring covaraites.

classes.name: modrover.strategies.full.Full

methods.name: modrover.strategies.full.Full.first_layer
documentation: Leanrer ids corresponding to the first layer.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.full.Full.second_layer
documentation: Leanrer ids corresponding to the second layer.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.full.Full.get_next_layer
documentation:
Find every single possible learner ID combination, return in a
single layer. If the {code}`curr_layer=first_layer`, it will return the
{code}`second_layer` and if the {code}`curr_layer=second_layer`, it will
return an empty layer. The first layer and the second layer together
cover all possible combinations.

# Parameters

curr_layer

: Current explored set of learner ids.

learners

: A dictionary contains all fitted learners.

Full strategy explore every possible covariate combinations.

Parameters
----------
num_covs
    Number of exploring covaraites.

classes.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy.base_learner_id
documentation: Starting learner id of the strategy

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy.get_next_layer
documentation:
Abstract method to generate the next set of learner ids.

Parameters
----------
curr_layer
    Current explored set of learner ids.
learners
    A dictionary contains all fitted learners.
**kwargs
    Other key word arguments.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy._get_upstream_learner_ids
documentation:
Given a learnerID, generate the upstream nodes.

Regardless of the selected strategy, we should be able to guarantee at
least one of the upstreams has already been visited if we are at a
particular node.

Note that this is going to search the opposite direction of the
specified strategy.  e.g. for DownExplore, the upstream IDs are going to
be parents

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy._as_learner_id
documentation: Validate the provided covariate_id set by the number of total covariates.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy._get_learner_id_children
documentation: Create a new set of child covariate id combinations based on the current one.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy._get_learner_id_parents
documentation: Create a parent LearnerID class with one less covariate than the current modelid.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.base.Strategy._filter_curr_layer
documentation:
Filter out low-performing covariate ids from selection.

Algorithm:
Select the n best performing covariate ids out of the input set
Drop if this candidate model performed worse than any of its parent
Return the remainder

An abstract base class representing a valid Rover strategy.

The strategy is responsible for selecting the next set of LearnerIDs,
determining the next layer of individual learners that Rover will fit.
Fitting learners and storing results is managed by Rover itself.

Parameters
----------
num_covs
    Number of exploring covaraites.

classes.name: modrover.strategies.forward.Forward

methods.name: modrover.strategies.forward.Forward.get_next_layer
documentation:
The forward strategy will select a set of learner IDs numbering one more
than the current. This function will return next set of learner ids
corresponding the learers that need to be fitted.

E.g. if the full set of ids is 1-5, and our current is (0, 1)

The children will be (0, 1, 2), (0, 1, 3), (0, 1, 4), (0, 1, 5)

Parameters
----------
curr_layer
    Current explored set of learner ids.
learners
    A dictionary contains all fitted learners.
min_improvement
    Minimum performance improvement requirement for the learner to be
    consider qualified to generate the next layer.
max_len
    Maximum number of learner in each layer.

methods.name: modrover.strategies.forward.Forward._get_upstream_learner_ids
documentation:
Return the possible previous nodes.
For Forward, this is the current learner id's parents.

Forward strategy starts from the model with only the {code}`cov_fixed`
and explores forwards with more and more covariates base on the learner
performance.

# Parameters

num_covs

: Number of exploring covaraites.

functions.name: modrover.learner._detach_df
documentation: Detach data and all the arrays from the regmod model.

classes.name: modrover.learner.Learner

methods.name: modrover.learner.Learner.coef
documentation: Coefficients in the regmod model.

methods.name: modrover.learner.Learner.vcov
documentation: Variance-covarianace matrix for the coefficients in the regmod model.

methods.name: modrover.learner.Learner.fit
documentation:
Fit a set of models on a series of holdout datasets.

This method will fit a model over k folds of the dataset, where k is the
length of the provided holdouts list. It is up to the user to decide the
train-test splits for each holdout column.

On each fold of the dataset, the trained model will predict out on the
validation set and obtain a evaluate. The averaged evaluate across all
folds becomes the model's overall score.

Finally, a model is trained with all data in order to generate the final
coefficients.

Parameters
----------
data
    A dataframe containing the training data
holdouts
    Which column names to iterate over for cross validation. If it is
    `None`, insample performance score will be used to evaluate the
    model.
**optimizer_options
    Extra options for the optimizer.

methods.name: modrover.learner.Learner.predict
documentation:
Generate prediction using regmod model. This function will return
predictions for the {code}`main_param` with given data.

# Parameters

data

: A dataset to generate predictions from

model

: A fitted RegmodModel. If it is `None`, will use the overall model
  rather than the cross-validation model.

return_ui

: If `return_ui=True`, a matrix will be returned. The first row
  is the point prediction, second and thrid rows are the lower and
  upper bound of the prediction.

alpha

: When `return_ui=True`, function will return (1 - `alpha`)
  uncertainty interval. By default, `alpha=0.05`.

methods.name: modrover.learner.Learner.evaluate
documentation:
Given a model and a test set, generate a performance score.

Score is based on the provided evaluation metric, comparing the
difference between observed and predicted values.

# Parameters

data

: The data set to generate predictions from

model

: The fitted model to set predictions on. If `None` will use the
  overall model rather than the cross-validation model.

Individual learner class for one specific covariate configuration.

# Parameters

model_class

: Regmod model constructor

obs

: Name corresponding to the observation column in the data frame

main_param

: The main parameter we are exploring. This is aligned with the {class}`modrover.rover.Rover` class.

param_specs

: Parameter settings for the regmod model

weights

: Name corresponding to the weights column in the data frame

get_score

: Function that evaluate the score of of the model

classes.name: modrover.rover.Rover

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.model_class
documentation: Model class that `model_type` refers to.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.params
documentation: A tuple of parameter names belong to the model class.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.variables
documentation:
A tuple of the variable names belong the model class with full list
of covariates.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.num_vars
documentation: Number of variables with full list of covariates.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.super_learner_id
documentation: Learner id for the super learner.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.super_learner
documentation: Ensembled super learner.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.learner_info
documentation: A data frame contains important information of fitted learners.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.summary
documentation:
A data frame contains the summary information of explored covariates
across all fitted learners.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.fit
documentation:
Fits the ensembled super learner.

Explores over all covariate slices as defined by the input strategy, and
fits the sublearners.

The super learner coefficients are determined by the ensemble method
parameters, and the super learner itself will be created - to be used in
prediction and summarization.

# Parameters

data

: Training data to fit individual learners on.

strategies

: The selection strategy to determine the model tree. Valid strategies
  include "forward", "backward" and "full".

strategy_options

: A dictionary with key as the strategy name and value as the option
  with calling the strategy. By default, `strategy_options=None` where
  all default options will be used by the strategies.

top_pct_score

: Only the learners with score that are greater or equal than
  `best_score * (1 - top_score)` can be selected. When
  `top_score = 0` only the best model will be selected.

top_pct_learner

: Only the best `top_pct_learner * num_learners` will be selected.

coef_bounds

: User pre-specified bounds for the coefficients. This is a dictionary
  with key as the covariate name and the value as the bounds. The
  learner will be marked valid or not if the coefficients are within
  the bounds. Invalid learners will not be used in ensemble process
  to create super learner. By default, `coef_bounds=None`, where
  there is not validation based on the value of the coefficients.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.predict
documentation:
Predict with ensembled super learner.

# Parameters

data

: Testing data to predict

return_ui

: If `return_ui=True`, a matrix will be returned. The first row
  is the point prediction, second and thrid rows are the lower and
  upper bound of the prediction.

alpha

: When `return_ui=True`, function will return (1 - `alpha`)
  uncertainty interval. By default, `alpha=0.05`.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover.plot
documentation:
Plot the result of the exploration. Each panel of the figure
corresponding to one covariate in the `cov_exploring`. We plot the
spread of the coefficients across all learners along with color
represents their performance score.

# Parameters

bins

: When `bins=None`, the coefficients will be spread along the y axis
  randomly to display the spread. When user pass in an integer, the
  x axis will be divided into bins and the y value will be assigned
  according to the ranking of score within the bin.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover._explore
documentation: Explore the entire tree of learners

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover._get_super_learner
documentation: Call at the end of fit, so model is configured at the end of fit.

methods.name: modrover.rover.Rover._get_super_coef
documentation:
Generates the weighted ensembled coefficients across all fitted
learners.

Rover class explores model space and creates final super learner for
prediction and inference.

# Parameters

model_type

: Type of the model. For example `"gaussian"` or `"poisson"`

obs

: The name of the column representing observations

cov_fixed

: A list representing the covariates are present in every learner

cov_exploring

: A list representing the covariates rover will explore over

main_param

: The main parameter where the `cov_fixed` and `cov_exploring` are
  applied to. By default `main_param=None`, and when the model only have
  one parameter, `main_param` will be automatically re-assigned to be
  that parameter. If we have multiple parameters in the model, user has to
  specify `main_param`.

param_specs

: Parameter settings including, link function, priors, etc

weights

: Column name corresponding to the weights for each data point

holdouts

: A list of column names containing 1's and 0's that represent folds in
  the rover cross-validation algorithm

get_score

: A callable used to evaluate cross-validated score of sub-learners
  in rover
